math_recheck: Re-derived against Adler 1969 (PR 177,2426) Eqs.(5),(17),(20),(22),(29),(30),(A1)-(A6) and Bell-Jackiw 1969 Eqs.(3.2)-(3.12),(4.10): anomaly coeff 8pi^2 and operator term (alpha0/4pi)eps FF == e0^2/16pi^2 eps FF confirmed; both code blocks run -> 8*pi**2. Found and fixed a dropped factor m in the sigma-model T(k^2) (Bell-Jackiw 3.6: 8*pi^2*g*m, giving T(0)=4pi^2 g/m, not g/m^2) and a flipped index ordering/sign in p_mu F^{sigma mu nu} (now -4pi^2 eps^{sigma alpha beta nu} p_beta q_alpha per BJ 3.12c).
fixes: reasoning.md:136 sigma-model triangle T(k^2)=8pi^2 g -> 8pi^2 g m (dropped mass factor; added 1/(2m^2) integral note) so T(0)=4pi^2 g/m is dimensionally correct (Bell-Jackiw Eq.3.6/3.7)
reasoning.md:148 & answer.md:50 p_mu F^{sigma mu nu} surface term: eps^{sigma alpha beta nu} p_alpha q_beta -> p_beta q_alpha to match Bell-Jackiw Eq.3.12c sign
reasoning.md:66 removed imported anecdote 'I told Treiman the result and he was as astonished as I was' (biographical/recounted, out of present-tense frame)
reasoning.md:105 'Steinberger/Bell-Jackiw puzzle' -> 'sigma-model puzzle, Steinberger's nonzero loop' (Bell-Jackiw is a target paper; dropped target-author citation, kept Steinberger prior art)
answer.md:56 'Bell-Jackiw's mass-dependent Pauli-Villars-Gupta couplings' -> 'mass-dependent Pauli-Villars-Gupta couplings' (removed target-author attribution of the sigma-model regulator route)
reasoning.md:121 trimmed pre-announcement 'This is the cleanest place to see both that the puzzle is real and that the surface term is the culprit' into in-frame motivation

context.md:47 Sutherland-Veltman gap reworded to remove answer leakage ('one of those steps secretly moves an integration variable inside a linearly divergent loop' -> generic 'no check that the underlying loop integrals are well-behaved enough')
